title: Waiters and waitresses
description: Perform a variety of food preparation duties other than cooking, such as preparing cold foods and shellfish, slicing meat, and brewing coffee or tea. Take orders and serve food and beverages to patrons at tables in dining establishment. Serve food to individuals outside of a restaurant environment, such as in hotel rooms, hospital rooms, residential care facilities, or cars. (Based on 11 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Remove trays and stack dishes for return to kitchen after meals are finished.
Describe and recommend wines to customers, and bring wine selections to tables with appropriate glasses, pouring the wines for customers.
Monitor food distribution to ensure that meals are delivered correctly and dietary guidelines are followed.
Inform customers of daily specials and explain how various menu items are prepared, describing ingredients and cooking methods while answering any questions about menu items.
Clean tables or counters after patrons have finished dining, and perform cleaning duties around the food service areas such as mopping, vacuuming, and taking out trash.
Prepare hot, cold, and mixed drinks for patrons, chill bottles of wine, and serve a variety of beverages including coffee, tea, and soft drinks.
Prepare food items such as salads, appetizers, or desserts, perform food preparation duties while portioning food, and create special dressings and sauces as condiments for sandwiches.
Stock service areas with supplies such as coffee, food, tableware, and linens, and maintain adequate inventory of clean linens, silverware, and dishes.
Fill condiment containers and prepare tables for meals, including setting up linens, silverware, and glassware.
Escort customers to their tables and greet and seat them, determining seating arrangements and providing information about local areas as needed.
Serve food or beverages to patrons, including assisting waiters or waitresses as required, and ensuring that customers are enjoying their meals, responding to any complaints.
Take food orders from patrons, relay orders to kitchens or serving counters, and record amounts served to customers.
Remove dishes and glasses from tables or counters and clean or sterilize dishes and kitchen utensils, including washing, peeling, and cutting various foods for preparation.
Load trays with accessories such as eating utensils, napkins, and condiments, and ensure that servings match orders or instructions placed on trays.
Examine trays to ensure they contain all required items and monitor food preparation techniques.
Prepare and serve specialty dishes at tables as required, providing a complete dining experience for patrons.
Sweep or scrub floors, clean garbage cans, and manage the cleanliness of kitchen and service areas regularly.
Assist with various tasks in the kitchen, supporting cooks and kitchen staff as needed.
Collect payments from customers and prepare checks that itemize and total meal costs and sales taxes.
Provide customers with information on routes, prices, and other inquiries especially relevant to their needs as diners or inquiring guests.
Perform additional tasks such as packaging take-out foods or serving food in a variety of dining environments besides traditional restaurants.
Assist with banquet table setups and meet any additional service requirements in large events.
Monitor stock levels and inform supervisors when supplies are low, placing orders as needed.
Transport supplies or equipment between storage and work areas, either by hand or using carts, ensuring efficient operation in the dining environment.
Maintain kitchen work areas and utensils in clean and orderly condition, including disinfecting processes when necessary.
Prepare meal trays according to patients' diets or specific customer preferences, adapting to their needs during service.
Place clean dishes, utensils, and cooking equipment back into storage to uphold sanitation standards.
Carry food, silverware, or linens to and from service areas, facilitating a smooth dining experience for customers.
Sort and remove trash, cleaning garbage containers and ensuring disposal complies with health regulations.
Maintain dining areas and serving stations stocked with ice, napkins, or other necessary items to provide guest readiness.
Prepare and package individual place settings or assist in organization for service flow in the dining area.
Perform inventory checks for kitchen and dining supplies to ensure everything necessary for service is readily available.
Operate in serving positions to support the overall restaurant experience, ensuring all tasks promote effective customer service.
Confer with customers, supervisors, or other personnel to exchange information or resolve problems.
Train workers in proper operational procedures and explain company policies.
Resolve customer complaints regarding performance or services rendered.
Enforce safety rules and regulations.

emerging_tasks.task: Check with customers to see if they want to apply any rewards to their purchase.
Perform routine tasks, such as refilling syrups, sanitizer bottles, and other essential supplies.
Check and log refrigerator, freezer, and cooler temperatures.
Clean and sanitize the dining area, including tables.
Prepare food, such as sandwiches, for customers.
